<output>
Here is the complete project structure report.

---

## 1. Top-Level Directory Structure

```
/home/user/workspace/
  .gitignore
  components/
    AppProvider.js
  next.config.ts
  node_modules/          (180 entries, installed)
  package-lock.json
  package.json
  pages/
    _app.js
    _document.js
    _error.js
    404.js
    index.js
    api/
      posts/
        [id].js
        index.js
    blog/
      [id].js
      index.js
  styles/
    globals.css
  tsconfig.json
```

There is **no `app/` directory** -- this project uses the Next.js **Pages Router**, not the App Router.

---

## 2. `pages/` Directory -- Every File and Subdirectory

```
pages/
  _app.js              # Custom App component (layout wrapper)
  _document.js         # Custom Document component (HTML shell)
  _error.js            # Custom global error page
  404.js               # Custom 404 "Not Found" page
  index.js             # Homepage "/" -- SSR via getServerSideProps
  blog/
    index.js           # "/blog" -- ISR via getStaticProps + revalidate
    [id].js            # "/blog/:id" -- SSG + getStaticPaths + getStaticProps with fallback: 'blocking'
  api/
    posts/
      index.js         # "/api/posts" -- GET (list), POST (create)
      [id].js          # "/api/posts/:id" -- GET, PUT, DELETE
```

---

## 3. `app/` Directory

**Does not exist.** This is a pure Pages Router project.

---

## 4. `package.json` -- Dependencies and Scripts

**Scripts:**
| Script | Command |
|--------|---------|
| `dev` | `next dev` |
| `build` | `next build` |
| `start` | `next start` |

**Dependencies (runtime):**
| Package | Version |
|---------|---------|
| `@ai-sdk/react` | `2.0.0-beta.28` |
| `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` | `^1.17.0` |
| `ai` | `5.0.59` |
| `next` | `16.2.0-canary.41` (canary build of Next.js 16) |
| `react` | `19.1.0` |
| `react-dom` | `19.1.0` |
| `zod` | `^4.0.10` |

**Dev Dependencies:**
| Package | Version |
|---------|---------|
| `@types/node` | `^20` |
| `@types/react` | `^19` |
| `@types/react-dom` | `^19` |
| `typescript` | `^5` |
| `vitest` | `^3.1.3` |
| `@vitejs/plugin-react` | `^4.4.1` |
| `vite-tsconfig-paths` | `^5.1.4` |

The project is marked `"type": "module"` (ESM).

Notable: The `ai` and `@ai-sdk/react` packages (Vercel AI SDK v5) plus `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` are present as dependencies, though they are not imported anywhere in the current source code.

---

## 5. `tsconfig.json`

Key settings:
- **Target:** `ES2017`
- **JSX:** `react-jsx`
- **Strict mode:** `true`
- **Module:** `esnext` with `bundler` module resolution
- **Path alias:** `@/*` maps to `./*` (project root)
- **Includes:** `next-env.d.ts`, all `**/*.ts`/`**/*.tsx`, `.next/types/**/*.ts`
- **Plugins:** Uses the `next` TypeScript plugin

---

## 6. `next.config.ts`

Minimal configuration -- just an empty object:

```ts
import type { NextConfig } from "next";
const nextConfig: NextConfig = {};
export default nextConfig;
```

---

## 7. Shared Layout / Component Files

### `components/AppProvider.js`

A React Context provider that wraps the entire app. It provides:
- `theme` state (defaults to `'light'`)
- `setTheme` setter
- A `useAppContext()` hook exported for consuming components

### `pages/_app.js`

The custom App component. It:
- Wraps all pages in `<AppProvider>`
- Imports global CSS from `../styles/globals.css`
- Renders a shared layout: `<header>` with a site title "My Blog" and nav links (Home, Blog), a `<main>` area for `<Component>`, and a `<footer>` with copyright.

### `pages/_document.js`

The custom Document component. It:
- Sets `<Html lang="en">`
- Injects meta description, favicon link, Google Fonts preconnect, and the Inter font stylesheet

### `styles/globals.css`

Global stylesheet covering:
- CSS reset (`*` box-sizing, margin/padding zero)
- Body typography (Inter font, line-height 1.6, color #333)
- `.app-container` centered max-width 1200px layout
- Header and nav styles
- `.posts-grid` responsive CSS grid for blog post cards
- `.post-card` with border and rounded corners
- `.comments` and `.comment` styles for the individual blog post page
- `.error-page` centered error display
- Global `button` styles (blue `#0070f3`, white text, rounded)

---

## 8. API Routes in `pages/api/`

Two API route files exist:

### `pages/api/posts/index.js` -- `/api/posts`

| Method | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `GET` | Returns a hardcoded array of 2 posts (simulated) |
| `POST` | Requires `title` and `content` in body; returns 400 if missing; otherwise returns a simulated new post with `Date.now()` id |
| Other | Returns `405 Method Not Allowed` with `Allow: GET, POST` header |

### `pages/api/posts/[id].js` -- `/api/posts/:id`

| Method | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `GET` | Returns a simulated single post object with the id from the URL |
| `PUT` | Accepts optional `title`/`content` in body; returns simulated updated post |
| `DELETE` | Returns a success message confirming deletion |
| Other | Returns `405 Method Not Allowed` with `Allow: GET, PUT, DELETE` header |

---

## Data Fetching Summary (Pages)

| Route | File | Data Method | External Source |
|-------|------|-------------|-----------------|
| `/` | `pages/index.js` | `getServerSideProps` | `jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts?_limit=5` |
| `/blog` | `pages/blog/index.js` | `getStaticProps` + ISR (60s) | `jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts` |
| `/blog/[id]` | `pages/blog/[id].js` | `getStaticPaths` (10 pre-rendered posts, `fallback: 'blocking'`) + `getStaticProps` (ISR 300s) | `jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/:id` + `/comments` |
| `/api/posts` | `pages/api/posts/index.js` | API Route (simulated data) | None (hardcoded) |
| `/api/posts/[id]` | `pages/api/posts/[id].js` | API Route (simulated data) | None (hardcoded) |
</output>

text:
Now I have a comprehensive picture of the project. Let me plan the migration from Pages Router to App Router:

**Files to migrate (all .js → .tsx/.ts):**

1. **`pages/_app.js`** → **`app/layout.tsx`** — The custom App component becomes the root layout
   - Wraps in `<AppProvider>`
   - Imports global CSS
   - Shared layout (header, nav, footer)

2. **`pages/_document.js`** → **`app/layout.tsx`** — Document settings go into the root layout
   - `<Html lang="en">` → `<html lang="en">` in layout
   - Meta description, favicon, Google Fonts
   - The `<Head>` component from next/document → metadata export

3. **`pages/_error.js`** → **`app/error.tsx`** — Error boundary for route segments
   - In App Router, this becomes `error.tsx` which is a client component

4. **`pages/404.js`** → **`app/not-found.tsx`** — Custom 404 page
   - In App Router, 404 is handled by `not-found.tsx`

5. **`pages/index.js`** → **`app/page.tsx`** — Homepage
   - `getServerSideProps` → Server Component with `fetch` (App Router pattern)

6. **`pages/blog/index.js`** → **`app/blog/page.tsx`**
   - `getStaticProps` with ISR → Server Component with `fetch` and `next: { revalidate: 60 }`

7. **`pages/blog/[id].js`** → **`app/blog/[id]/page.tsx`**
   - `getStaticPaths` + `getStaticProps` → `generateStaticParams` + Server Component with `fetch` and `next: { revalidate: 300 }`

8. **`pages/api/posts/index.js`** → **`app/api/posts/route.ts`**
   - API route using Route Handlers (`NextRequest`, `NextResponse`)

9. **`pages/api/posts/[id].js`** → **`app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts`**
   - API route using Route Handlers

10. **`components/AppProvider.js`** → **`components/AppProvider.tsx`** — Convert to TypeScript

11. **`styles/globals.css`** — Keep as-is, but import from the root layout

12. **`next.config.ts`** — May need updates for App Router (but minimal config is fine)

Let me read all the source files now to understand their exact content before creating the App Router equivalents.
Excellent! I now have a complete picture of the project. Let me read all the source files to understand their exact content before migrating.

The build succeeded. Let me analyze the output:

1. **`/`** — `ƒ` (Dynamic) — This is because the homepage uses `headers()` which makes it dynamic. This matches the original behavior where it used `getServerSideProps` (SSR). ✓

2. **`/_not-found`** — `○` (Static) — The static not-found page. ✓

3. **`/api/posts`** — `ƒ` (Dynamic) — API route. ✓

4. **`/api/posts/[id]`** — `ƒ` (Dynamic) — API route with dynamic params. ✓

5. **`/blog`** — `○` (Static) — Prerendered as static, revalidates every 1m (ISR from `next: { revalidate: 60 }`). This matches the original `getStaticProps` with `revalidate: 60`. ✓

6. **`/blog/[id]`** — `●` (SSG) — Prerendered via `generateStaticParams`, revalidates every 5m. The `fallback: 'blocking'` behavior is preserved since `dynamicParams` defaults to `true`, so non-pre-generated paths are server-rendered on first request. ✓

text:
## Migration Complete ✅

The entire application has been migrated from Pages Router to App Router. The `pages/` directory has been removed. The build compiles successfully with all routes properly recognized.

### Migration Summary

| Pages Router (removed) | App Router (created) | Key Changes |
|---|---|---|
| `pages/_app.js` + `pages/_document.js` | `app/layout.tsx` | Root layout with `metadata` export, `next/font/google` for the Inter font, `AppProvider` wrapper, shared header/nav/footer |
| `pages/_error.js` | `app/error.tsx` | Client component error boundary (`'use client'`) with `error` and `reset` props |
| `pages/404.js` | `app/not-found.tsx` | Uses `Link` instead of `useRouter().push` |
| `pages/index.js` | `app/page.tsx` | Async server component — `headers()` from `next/headers` replaces `getServerSideProps` `req`, inline `fetch` replaces prop-based data |
| `pages/blog/index.js` | `app/blog/page.tsx` | `fetch` with `{ next: { revalidate: 60 } }` replaces `getStaticProps` + ISR |
| `pages/blog/[id].js` | `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx` | `generateStaticParams` replaces `getStaticPaths`, `generateMetadata` for dynamic `<title>`, `loading.tsx` replaces `router.isFallback` check, `params` is typed as `Promise<>` (Next.js 16 async params) |
| `pages/api/posts/index.js` | `app/api/posts/route.ts` | Exported `GET`/`POST`/`OPTIONS` functions using `NextRequest`/`NextResponse` |
| `pages/api/posts/[id].js` | `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts` | Exported `GET`/`PUT`/`DELETE`/`OPTIONS` with typed `params: Promise<{ id: string }>` |
| `components/AppProvider.js` | `components/AppProvider.tsx` | Converted to TypeScript with `AppContextType` interface, `ReactNode` children type |

### App Router APIs Used
- **`metadata` export** — static/dynamic SEO via `generateMetadata`
- **`headers()`** — async headers access in server components
- **`next/font/google`** — self-hosted Inter font (no external CDN requests)
- **`generateStaticParams`** — static path generation with `dynamicParams: true` (default, matching `fallback: 'blocking'`)
- **`loading.tsx`** — streaming/suspense loading state
- **`error.tsx`** — client component error boundary
- **`not-found.tsx`** — 404 page
- **Route Handlers** — `NextRequest`/`NextResponse` with exported HTTP method functions
- **Async `params`** — `params: Promise<...>` pattern for Next.js 16

### Build Output Routes
```
/              → Dynamic (SSR — uses headers())
/_not-found    → Static
/api/posts     → Dynamic
/api/posts/[id]→ Dynamic
/blog          → Static (ISR, revalidates every 1m)
/blog/[id]     → SSG (10 paths pre-built, revalidates every 5m, dynamicParams for others)
```
